title: Desktop App Launch Control & Readiness Pattern
summary: Pattern for managing desktop application launch lifecycle: startup guard (single-instance lock), subprocess health monitoring with readiness polling, graceful shutdown sequencing, and crash recovery via lock file cleanup. The pattern separates launch orchestration from business logic, with a main overlay script that coordinates service initialization, health checks, and teardown in a defined order.
why_reusable: Any long-running Windows desktop tool or assistant needs reliable startup guard, process health monitoring, and clean shutdown. The pattern of readiness polling with timeout, combined with operation lock files, generalizes to tray apps, automation agents, voice assistants, and system utilities.
adapter_target: Generic desktop assistant / Windows tool / agent app — adapter should be a single-file launch controller that can be imported by any desktop entry point.
implementation_notes: Use a lock file (e.g. %TEMP%\app.lock) with PID to detect stale instances
Poll readiness via health endpoint or internal flag with configurable timeout and interval
Shutdown sequence: stop accepting new work → drain queues → close subprocess → release lock
Separate launch orchestration from service logic — the overlay script should only coordinate
On Windows, handle both graceful close (WM_CLOSE) and forceful termination
tests_required: Unit test: lock file acquisition and release
Unit test: stale lock detection and cleanup
Integration test: subprocess launch with readiness polling timeout
Integration test: graceful shutdown with active subprocess
Integration test: crash recovery on next launch
risks: Lock file must be cleaned up on crash — stale locks block relaunch
PowerShell subprocess may hang on exit; always set a timeout
Readiness polling can race with service initialization; use a flag, not just process alive check
Multiple instances detection must work with both normal and elevated processes
do_not_do: Do not block the main thread during readiness polling
Do not hardcode paths in the launch controller
Do not skip the stale lock detection — crashed previous instance is common on Windows
Do not assume subprocess exit means clean shutdown; check exit code
packaging_impact: Lock file and subprocess management add no packaging weight; psutil optional
notes: Derived from xiaohuang voice_overlay.py orchestration patterns. This is an internal project asset — the pattern is the reusable artifact, not the source code.
